aggregate_brief: Today's standouts: GB Power — 90th-percentile of 5-yr range — historically high; EU Storage — 14.0 pp below the 5-yr seasonal average.

news.geopolitics_summary: EU-US trade tensions (Trump tariffs, June 16 vote) threaten transatlantic accord; Middle East conflict drives energy crisis; ETS under July review amid Polish pushback.

themes.headline: ETS review due July; Poland criticizes 'insane' EU climate policy framework
themes.why_it_matters: EUA volatility risk. July Commission review of Emissions Trading System will reshape carbon price signal; Poland's political opposition signals possible floor/ceiling tweaks, affecting EUA trading ranges and power-carbon spark.

themes.headline: Commission exempts green investments from EU spending rules amid energy crisis
themes.why_it_matters: Accelerates capex for wind/solar/interconnects. Fiscal flexibility for green builds reduces RES cost of capital, pressuring long-term power prices (Cal+2/+3) and supporting low-carbon baseload relative to gas.

themes.headline: EU trade lawmakers back Trump accord; final plenary vote scheduled June 16
themes.why_it_matters: Potential tariff escalation (10% forced-labor levy proposed) could disrupt LNG capex cycles, trade finance, and transatlantic energy investment. Enactment risk on June 16 adds near-term volatility to Cal+1 curve via sentiment.

themes.headline: El Niño to intensify heat and climate disasters in coming months, UN warns
themes.why_it_matters: Higher ambient temperatures reduce thermal plant efficiency and increase cooling demand; boost AC load in summer curve. Hydropower generation risk if dry conditions persist; TTF upside if gas-to-power switching needed.

themes.headline: US natural gas power consumption near highs this summer; record 2027 expected
themes.why_it_matters: Higher US gas demand tightens LNG export margins and supports US export prices (Henry Hub + transport). If US gas tightness rises, LNG cargoes divert from Europe or TTF premiums compress; indirect upside for EU power via gas scarcity.

watchlist: June 16: EU trade accord final plenary vote; assess tariff escalation impact on LNG/green capex.
July: Commission ETS review publication; watch for floor/ceiling, scope changes (potential EUA spike).
Monitor Middle East conflict spillovers into oil/LNG supply disruptions and EU fuel-switching behavior.
Track El Niño intensity; watch European summer hydropower forecasts and cooling-demand forecasts.
